steps.title: Distribute the monomial to each term
steps.content:
We're multiplying $x^{-6}y^3z^5$ by a binomial, so distribute the monomial to each term:
$$(x^{-6}y^3z^5)(x^4z^5 + y^8z^{-7}) = (x^{-6}y^3z^5)(x^4z^5) + (x^{-6}y^3z^5)(y^8z^{-7})$$

steps.title: Apply exponent rules to each product
steps.content:
When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents.

First term: $x^{-6+4}y^3z^{5+5} = x^{-2}y^3z^{10}$

Second term: $x^{-6}y^{3+8}z^{5+(-7)} = x^{-6}y^{11}z^{-2}$

steps.title: Combine results to find the answer
steps.content: The product is $x^{-2}y^3z^{10} + x^{-6}y^{11}z^{-2}$, which matches choice D — D is correct.
